steps.title: Simplify the given line
steps.content: Start with $3(x-n)=9y+15n$. Expanding gives $3x-3n=9y+15n$, so $3x-9y=18n$. Divide by $3$ to get $$x-3y=6n.$$

steps.title: Use a representative positive n
steps.content: Because $n$ is positive but not fixed, choose the easy value $n=1$ for a Desmos check. The original line becomes $x-3y=6$. Choice D becomes $x-3y=3$.

steps.title: Fit both lines from two points
steps.content: Use two points on each line and run linear regressions. For $x-3y=6$, the points $(0,-2)$ and $(3,-1)$ fit $y=\frac{1}{3}x-2$. For choice D, the points $(0,-1)$ and $(3,0)$ fit $y=\frac{1}{3}x-1$. Same slope, different intercepts means parallel distinct lines.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content: Choice A is exactly $x-3y=6n$, the same line as the original, so it would give infinitely many solutions. Choices B and C have $x+3y$, so their slope is different and they intersect the original. Choice D has the same left side but a different constant, so it is the no-solution choice. Choice D is correct.
